commands.review_summary:
The PR redacts plaintext provider apiKey values before serializing generated models.json and adds regression coverage for runtime-config and existing models.json plaintext keys.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current merge mode preserves existing models.json apiKey values, while the PR redacts those values after merge. I did not run the write path because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: This needs contributor proof plus a maintainer security/compatibility decision before any repair or merge automation should proceed.

target.title: fix(models): redact plaintext provider api keys
target.body:
Fixes #11829

## Summary
- Redacts plaintext provider apiKey values before serializing models.json.
- Keeps recognized non-secret markers such as OPENAI_API_KEY and secretref-managed unchanged.
- Adds regression coverage for runtime-config secrets and stale plaintext keys carried forward from an existing models.json.

## Compatibility note
This intentionally replaces plaintext keys found in the generated catalog with the managed secret marker so prompt-visible model metadata does not persist credentials. Auth should continue to come from runtime/source config or auth profiles.

## Validation
- node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts src/agents/models-config.merge.test.ts src/agents/model-auth-markers.test.ts
- pnpm tsgo:core
- pnpm tsgo:core:test
- git diff --check
target.author: zr9959

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
